originalTitle: Slot says VAR has gone against Liverpool - is he right?
originalDescription: Liverpool's exit from the Champions League was hastened by a VAR intervention. BBC Sport analyses the key refereeing decisions in their season.

originalTitle: Are Kompany's Bayern a glimpse at football after Guardiola?
originalDescription: With Vincent Kompany making an impressive impact at Bayern Munich, BBC Sport's tactics expert Umir Irfan takes a closer look at the manager's unique style.

originalTitle: Bayern links and World Cup hopes - a big summer for Gordon
originalDescription: Anthony Gordon faces a big summer - with World Cup hopes and the possibility that Newcastle will need to sell him. So what comes next for the England forward?

originalTitle: Liverpool fear lengthy lay-off for Ekitike
originalDescription: France forward Hugo Ekitike is expected to miss the World Cup after suffering a suspected Achilles injury during Liverpool's Champions League defeat by Paris St-Germain.

originalTitle: Two fans get first banning orders for tailgating
originalDescription: Two men become the first in England to be convicted for breaking a new tailgating law at last month's Carabao Cup final.

originalTitle: Refereeing v Atletico 'a robbery' - Barca's Raphinha
originalDescription: Barcelona's exit from the Champions League quarter-finals was "a robbery", says their forward Raphinha.

originalTitle: John Terry and Colchester - why now, and why are ex-pros buying clubs?
originalDescription: BBC Sport explores John Terry's imminent takeover of Colchester, why ex-pros are buying clubs, and what the feeling is among fans.

originalTitle: Liverpool boss Slot 'surprised' Hillsborough Law not yet passed
originalDescription: The Liverpool manager said families "should not have to fight for the truth" of how loved ones died.

originalTitle: Man Utd latest: Who plays at CB against Chelsea with no Maguire or Martinez?
British doubles legend Jamie Murray retires from tennis
originalDescription: "My tennis journey comes to an end" - Jamie Murray, who became the first British doubles player to rise to world No 1, has announced his retirement from tennis.

originalTitle: Could West Brom be punished for PSR breach, will it impact relegation battle?
originalDescription: With West Brom having been charged with an alleged breach of the EFL's Profit and Sustainability (PSR) rules, Sky Sports News' football correspondent Rob Dorsett explains the current situation the Baggies find themselves in.

originalTitle: Peaty wins British 100m title as he sets sights on Los Angeles 2028
originalDescription: Three-time Olympic champion Adam Peaty won the 100 metres breaststroke at the Aquatic Great Britain Swimming Championships on Tuesday as he sets his sights on the Los Angeles 2028 Olympics.

originalTitle: Hiku signs contract extension with Hull KR
Itoje: England can achieve 'special things' under Borthwick
originalDescription: Maro Itoje has reaffirmed his support for Steve Borthwick, insisting England can achieve "special things" under his leadership despite an abject Six Nations.

originalTitle: Live on Sky: Blackburn vs Coventry
Milan's San Siro could host Wimbledon tune-up event on grass from 2028
originalDescription: Italy will host a grass-court ATP 250 Wimbledon tune-up event in Milan starting in 2028 - and it could be played at Milan's San Siro stadium.
